title: The Jute Packaging Materials (Compulsory Use in Packing Commodities) Act, 1987
full_text: The Jute Packaging Materials (Compulsory Use in Packing Commodities) Act, 1987 जूट पैकेज सामग्री (वस्‍तु पैकिंग अनिवार्य प्रयोग)अधिनियम, 1987 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 198710 Act Number: 10 Enactment Date: 1987-05-09 Act Year: 1987 Short Title: The Jute Packaging Materials (Compulsory Use in Packing Commodities) Act, 1987 Hindi Title: जूट पैकेज सामग्री (वस्‍तु पैकिंग अनिवार्य प्रयोग)अधिनियम, 1987 Long Title: An Act to provide for the compulsory use of jute packaging material in the supply and distribution of certain commodities in the interests of production of raw jute and jute packaging material, and of persons engaged in the production thereof, and for matters connected therewith. Ministry: Ministry of Textiles Enforcement Date: 14-05-1987 Notification: 14th May, 1987, vide notification No. S.O. 467(E), dated 14th May, 1987, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3 (ii). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles The Jute Packaging Materials (Compulsory Use in Packing Commodities) Act, 1987 Show all section Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Power to specify commodities which are required to be packed in jute packaging material. Show Related Subordinates Section 4. Constitution of Standing Advisory Committee. Show Related Subordinates Section 5. Prohibition of packing in any material other than the jute packaging material. Section 6. Power to call for information and samples. Section 7. Power to enter and inspect. Section 8. Power to search and seize. Section 9. Penalty for contravention of section 5. Section 10. Penalty for false statement, etc. Section 11. Offences by companies. Section 12. Offences to be cognizable. Section 13. Power to delegate. Section 14. Central Government to give directions. Section 15. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 16. Power to exempt. Show Related Subordinates Section 17. Power to make rules.
